LB: Snowy limestone scarps above a narrow shelf; steep ridges between faulted interior basins
LC: Twin volcanic spires above rainforest; Atlantic reefs versus calmer leeward Caribbean bays
LI: Alpine valley trench; scree on calcareous peaks above terrace farms on steep slopes
LK: Gneiss highlands in monsoon clouds; terraced scarps down to lateritic coastal plains
LR: Mangrove estuaries on tidal flats; evergreen foothills on deeply weathered shield rock
LS: Basalt-capped plateau with alpine snow belts; dissected valleys on high interior scarps
LT: Lagoon bars behind wandering dunes; meltwater lake chains on flat glacial till inland
LU: Northern sandstone gorges; southern forest ridges on red beds above limestone scarps
LV: Glacial outwash plains; inland peat bogs behind shallow gulf shores and lake strings
LY: Sand seas and hamada over pediments; narrow green coastal range on limestone sea cliffs
MA: Atlas snowfields above steppe; wind-built ergs on dry hamada farther south
MC: Limestone terraces to pocket harbor; cliff-girt shore on a narrow mid-sea shelf
MD: Loess uplands with rolling fields; broad river terraces opening eastward to steppe plains
ME: Limestone massifs plunge to deep submarine canyons; polje basins between high alpine cols
MG: Basalt plateau with granite inselbergs; humid eastern escarpment above dry western savanna
MH: Ring reefs around lagoon basins; low islets on ramparts above pale lagoon floors
MK: Fault river trench through basins; forested ranges with snowy limestone ridge crests
ML: Wide basin with braided Sahel channels; dunes on lateritic pediments west of scrub
MM: Monsoonal lake chains in rain-shadow basins; braided megafans between forested fold ridges
MN: Cold steppe basins; gravel pavements rimmed by distant snow-capped continental ranges
MR: Atlantic shelf behind hammada; interior shifting dunes on stained basement plateaus
MT: Limestone islets on wide shelf; stepped scarps, maquis macchia, saline karst hollows
MU: Extinct-volcano shield with rainward rim; fringing reef lagoon on a broad submarine bank
MV: Sand cays on double reef rings; shallow lagoon floors over subsiding volcanic foundations
MW: Rift lake between escarpments; montane forest scarps above shelving shoreline plains
